messages.content:
The inventory investment metric that respond to following question: “How many times in a year the inventory was purchased and then sold?” is named:

The inventory turnover.

The inventory weeks of supply.

The inventory days of supply.

The day’s sales in inventory.
The inventory turnover.

Which of the following is not a qualitative forecasting technique?

Delphi Method.

Executive opinion.

Market research.

Time-series analysis.
Time-series analysis.

Which of the following forecasting method need stationarity in time series?

Moving Average method.

Weighted Moving Average method

Exponential Smoothing method.

All of the above.
Exponential Smoothing method.

hich of the following about Johnson’s rule is false?

Johnson’s rule does not give the value of make-span.

Johnson’s rule gives one optimal solution.

Johnson’s rule is applied for flow shop situation with deterministic processing time.

Johnson’s rule gives an approximate solution.
Johnson’s rule is applied for flow shop situation with deterministic processing time. (This statement is true, not false.)

Which of the following statements about Just-in-Time is correct?

JIT is essential for a company involved in project work.

JIT pushes inventory through the operations process.

JIT is only useful in a high product variety environment.

JIT seeks to reduce inventory in an effort to reduce waste.
JIT seeks to reduce inventory in an effort to reduce waste.

Which of the following statement is false?

CONWIP system is a pull production system without cards.

CONWIP is a Kanban system between the first process and the last process.

In CONWIP, card is assigned to production line and are not part number specific.

Both pull production and push production are used in CONWIP system.

CONWIP is a Kanban system between the first process and the last process. (This statement is false because CONWIP is a pull production system without using cards, unlike Kanban which is a pull production system that uses cards.)

n production planning, the feasibility of the proposed master schedule is assessed using the:

aggregate plan.

rough-cut capacity plan.

material plan.

available-to-promise inventory.
rough-cut capacity plan.

Which of the following aggregate planning strategies is likely to have the least impact on quality?

changing inventory level.

varying production rates through overtime.

subcontracting.

using part-time workers.
changing inventory level.

Which of the following is a factor that can affect the location selection?

Proximity to markets

Proximity to raw materials and suppliers

Labor costs

All other choices
All of the choices can affect location selection.

Which of the following statement is desirable in designing a process?

Fewer defect at process that are at the beginning of the manufacturing steps

Fewer defect at process that are in the middle of the manufacturing steps

Fewer defect at process that are near the end of the manufacturing steps

None of the above
Fewer defect at process that are near the end of the manufacturing steps.

Which of the following is a characteristic of large unit load?

Decreases work-in-process inventory

Support JIT production

Require bigger and heavier equipment

All other choices
Require bigger and heavier equipment.

Which of the following is a Layout Design Consideration?

Higher utilization of space, equipment, and people

Improved flow of information, materials, or people

Flexibility

All other choices
All of the choices are layout design considerations.

Who of the following is mainly responsible for determining HOW the product is to be produced?

Product designer

Business owner

Process designer

Schedule designer
Process designer.
